title: Biggest IELTS & UK Higher Education Expo 2026
details: Hosted by Smart BeeE, the Biggest IELTS & UK Higher Education Expo 2026 brings together official representatives from leading UK universities, expert counsellors, and aspiring students under one roof. The event is designed to help students discover suitable courses, understand English requirements, and evaluate institutional eligibility criteria (academic background, intake deadlines, and profile strength). Visitors can access direct profile assessments, submit applications on the spot, and explore realistic scholarship opportunities.

subEvents.title: University Delegate Consultations & On-the-Spot Assessment
subEvents.details: Connect directly with UK university representatives for real-time file evaluations, quick offer letters, and immediate application processing.

subEvents.title: Exclusive Expo Offers & Giveaways
subEvents.details: Includes opportunities for scholarships up to £10,000, 100% cashback on IELTS fees, a free air ticket, and free accommodation (terms and conditions apply). General perks include a complimentary visitor gift pack, hourly Fantasy Kingdom ticket vouchers, and a free laptop for the first 10 eligible students.

title: National STEAM Carnival
venue: Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology (BUET), Dhaka
fee: Olympiads: 200 BDT; Creativity Exhibition: 200 BDT (Each); Photography: 300 BDT. Stall Packages: General (BDT 10,000) / Food (BDT 15,000) / Booth (BDT 35,000)Contests.
details: Organized by Nexorah, the National STEAM Carnival at BUET is a multi-day event designed for curious learners, students, and skill-builders. It spans multiple disciplines across STEAM fields and caters to various age groups from Elementary (Play - Class 1) through Senior (Class 11 - Undergrad). Prizes include medals, crests, certificates, and gifts for the top performers. The 3-day national carnival is projected to host 3,000+ audience members and 50+ institutions, engaging youth aged 6 to 25Contests.

subEvents.title: Olympiads
subEvents.details: Subject-based competitive exams spanning Physics, Chemistry, Biology, General Science, ICT, Earth Science, and Math.

subEvents.title: Creativity Exhibition
subEvents.details: Includes Project Displays, Wall Magazines, Scrapbook Displays, Web Design & Development showcases, and Craftwork (for Elementary to Junior categories).

subEvents.title: Art & Photography
subEvents.details: Features an Art Exhibition and Art Competition (Elementary to Junior), alongside Mobile and DSLR Photography contests, and Instant Story Writing.

subEvents.title: Curiosity Zone
subEvents.details: Features interactive workshops alongside a theme-based food stall and a merchandise stall.

subEvents.title: Stall & Booth PartnershipsContests
subEvents.details: Brands and institutions can book stall spaces until August 9, 2026, for brand visibility among students and parentsContests. Options include 6x6 ft General Stalls for BDT 10,000, 8x8 ft Food Stalls for BDT 15,000, and 10x10 ft Booths for BDT 35,000[cite: 2]. Packages include on-site banner placements, social media static posts, and mentions in the post-event highlight video across the 3-day durationContests.

title: Cloud Technical Series: Agentic AI Edition
details: The Cloud Technical Series: Agentic AI Edition is a definitive virtual event focusing on the blueprint for building AI agents capable of handling millions of interactions. The sessions provide a battle-tested guide diving deep into top agent design patterns, complex build-versus-buy trade-offs, and orchestrating diverse models (including Gemini, Claude, and open source) to deliver peak performance at scale. Attendees will learn how to seamlessly transition from early prototypes to reliable, trustworthy global production systems.

subEvents.title: Orchestrating Enterprise-Grade AI Agents
subEvents.details: Learn how to orchestrate diverse models while managing costs and performance. The session covers building a unified architecture utilizing a single endpoint to switch models via simple configurations, backed by proven evaluation frameworks.

subEvents.title: Agentic Data Cloud & Gemini Enterprise
subEvents.details: Explore advancements in the Agentic Data Cloud to streamline workflows, including Universal Context and Multimodal AI Ready Data. A high-level demo will also showcase the transformative power of pairing Gemini Enterprise with Google Workspace to eliminate operational friction and boost cross-functional collaboration.

subEvents.title: Bridging the "Last Mile" Security Gap
subEvents.details: Address the stakes of security and compliance for autonomous agents. Learn from a retrospective of recurring failure patterns identified across over 70 real-world engagements, and explore core principles of identity, authentication, and authorization for agents.

title: Idea Competition 2026
details: Organized by the Innovation, Creativity & Entrepreneurship (ICE) Center at the University of Dhaka under the 'Capacity Building of Universities in Bangladesh to Promote Youth Entrepreneurship' project, in partnership with the UGC and MoE, and supported by KOICA Bangladesh and a South Korean PMC team. The competition aims to identify innovative, impactful, and commercially viable ideas/solutions from university students and alumni across Bangladesh and guide them into impact enterprises. Shortlisted applicants enter an Entrepreneurship Education and Development program.

subEvents.title: Training, Mentorship & Incubation Phase
subEvents.details: The 50 shortlisted applicants (batch 01) receive a complimentary 318 hours of training, mentorship, and industry experience program leading to Certificates and Credentials.

subEvents.title: Seed Funding & Prize Valuation
subEvents.details: Upon successful completion of the training, the Top 14 ideas stand to access a BDT 55 Lac seed funding pool: 1st Prize (BDT 10 lac), 2nd Prize (BDT 6 lac), 3rd Prize (BDT 5 lac), and 4th to 14th Prizes (BDT 3 lac each). Additional prizes will be awarded to the top 3 selected applicants, alongside need-based allowances and strategic stakeholder networking support.

links.Form PDF: doc/files/IdeaContests/Idea Competition 2026 for EDP Application Form (Batch 01).pdf

title: raylib 6.x gamejam
details: Welcome to raylib 6.x gamejam! Hosted officially by Ray, this game jam challenges developers to showcase their most amazing game creations utilizing the newly launched milestone release of raylib 6.x. Celebrating the next evolution of the simple, easy-to-use C game programming library, the event encourages raw, spartan-programmer style coding and cross-platform creative execution within a defined timeframe.

subEvents.title: Core Development & Submission Window
subEvents.details: Build and submit your game within the strict jam timeline. Submissions must explicitly focus on implementation using the new raylib 6.x features, hooks, and backend optimizations.

title: i-BIOHACKATHON 2026: SOLVING BIOLOGICAL QUESTIONS WITH AI & BIOINFORMATICS
details: Organized by INBIOSIS (Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia), i-BIOHACKATHON 2026 challenges students and industry professionals to apply data, AI tools, and critical thinking to transform precision biology and sustainability. Operating entirely without wet labs, teams of 2–4 members tackle real-world biological mysteries. Features a total prize pool of RM4,000 distributed as RM1,000 per winning track category. Registration closes on July 26, 2026.

subEvents.title: Bio-Explorer Track (Secondary School)
subEvents.details: Pitch innovative ideas focused on the future landscape of AI-Powered Agricultural Biotechnology.

subEvents.title: Bio-Analyst Track (Undergraduate)
subEvents.details: Analyze critical Bagworm data to devise protective solutions for Malaysian Oil Palm cultivation.

subEvents.title: Systems Biologist Track (Postgraduate)
subEvents.details: Integrate complex multi-omics data sets to crack the structural prawn growth mystery.

subEvents.title: Solutions Engineer Track (Industry)
subEvents.details: Develop production-ready pipelines targeting biomarkers and structural probiotics workflows.

title: Techathon Nationals & Rover Summit 2026
details: Organized by the IUT Robotics Society (IUTRS) at the Islamic University of Technology, Techathon Nationals & Rover Summit 2026 serves as a massive milestone platform bringing together robotics enthusiasts, student innovators, and engineering minds from all over Bangladesh. The tech fest fields diverse technical, creative, and gaming segments alongside a highly anticipated collective prize pool distribution across multiple structural tracks.

subEvents.title: Rover Summit & Core Engineering Showcase
subEvents.details: Bangladesh's first-ever Rover Summit featuring premier national rover teams collaborating, exchanging knowledge, and conducting technical showcases. Includes specialized robotics and engineering segments such as Project Showcasing, Poster Presentation, and RoboCAD design challenges.

subEvents.title: Robotics Competitions
subEvents.details: High-octane robotics tracks featuring SoccerBot, Line Follower Robot (LFR), Death Race, Drone Fury, and Battle Bot matchups testing control hardware, engineering constraints, and design execution.

subEvents.title: Academic & Innovation Tracks
subEvents.details: Problem-solving and strategic thinking environments including a Hackathon, STEM & Robo Olympiad, Mega Quiz, BizComp, and the upcoming Shark Tank University entrepreneurship layout.

subEvents.title: Gaming Segments
subEvents.details: Competitive esports segments featuring Valorant, Fifa 26, and eFootball Mobile tracks running alongside the main technical events.

title: BDSec CTF 2026
details: Organized annually by Knight Squad, a prominent cybersecurity team from Bangladesh, BDSec CTF is a premier international event open to global participants ranging from newcomers to seasoned professionals. The competition design seamlessly balances approachable, beginner-friendly entry barriers with advanced, high-scaling infrastructure puzzles to test problem-solving endurance under pressure.

subEvents.title: Core Competition Tracks
subEvents.details: A 24-hour international Jeopardy-style competition challenging participants across key domains including Web Exploitation, Pwn (Binary Exploitation), Reverse Engineering, Cryptography, Forensics/DFIR, and OSINT.

title: International AI Builders Congress 2026
venue: Daffodil International University, Ashulia Campus, Dhaka, Bangladesh
fee: Free / Variable based on journey tracks
details: Hosted by CloudCamp Bangladesh, the International AI Builders Congress 2026 is a premier structural execution platform designed to transition developers, innovators, and students from localized prototypes into production-grade global solutions. The comprehensive initiative focuses on key application scopes including product building, economic development, and job creation by bridging the gap between local talent and Non-Resident Bangladeshi (NRB) technology practitioners.

subEvents.title: Program Schedule
subEvents.details: Spans a multi-month structural continuum starting from onboarding sessions, routine technical check-ins, validation sprints, and interactive global mentorship labs, culminating in an expansive physical global gathering on December 26, 2026 (7:00 AM – 7:30 PM).

subEvents.title: Domains & Challenges
subEvents.details: Features structured development tasks running across 10 strategic technical domains and 50 global grand challenges. Highlighted areas focus on shipping secure, responsible, and enterprise-grade tools across healthcare, finance, legal networks, climate intelligence, agricultural modeling, civic frameworks, commerce, and fundamental AI infrastructure.

subEvents.title: Judging Criteria
subEvents.details: Solutions undergo strict evaluation across core metrics: Innovation & Originality (20%), Technical Execution & Structural Integrity (20%), Business Model & Global Sustainability Pathways (20%), alongside Measurable Real-World Impact & Ethical Compliance Protocols (20%).

subEvents.title: Phases & Stages
subEvents.details: Divided into progressive execution horizons: Phase 1 maps initial team configuration, problem identification, and technical onboarding. Phase 2 covers architecture build-outs and product development sprints under global practitioners. Phase 3 drives deployment testing, real-user validations, and international scalability readiness leading up to the final showcases.

title: MarXcellence 2026 (International Inter-University Digital Marketing Case Competition)
venue: North South University (NSU Main Auditorium) | Hybrid format for international participants
details: Presented by Dhaka Bank PLC, Co-Sponsored by Transcom Group, and Powered by Vatika Naturals, MarXcellence 2026 is Bangladesh's premier hybrid digital marketing case tournament hosted by NSU MIBC. Open globally to undergraduate student teams (2 to 4 members; cross-university teams fully permitted). Features a comprehensive BDT 520,000 total prize pool breakdowns: Champions receive BDT 225,000; 1st Runners-Up receive BDT 150,000; 2nd Runners-Up receive BDT 100,000; and the remaining 3 finalists receive BDT 15,000 each. Registration closes on July 8, 2026 (11:59 PM).

subEvents.title: Workshop 1 (Orientation & Foundational Knowledge)
subEvents.details: Held on 10th July, 2026. Led by digital marketing professionals. Focuses on the practical application of digital marketing tools, data collection fundamentals, and marketing analytics to equip teams for the opening case.

subEvents.title: Round 1: The Xploration Phase
subEvents.details: An intensive online case-solving segment where teams tackle a real-world digital marketing case problem. A select number of top-tier submissions will advance to the subsequent tier.

subEvents.title: Workshop 2 (Advanced Strategy & Corporate Networking)
subEvents.details: Led by industry veterans and corporate marketing experts. Breaks down complex branding mechanics, cross-channel promotional execution, and consumer behavioral research while serving as a direct networking gateway.

subEvents.title: Semi-Final: The Xpression Studio
subEvents.details: Qualifying teams must produce a comprehensive digital marketing layout paired alongside a supporting Online Video Commercial (OVC). Domestic teams present live at the NSU campus; international teams present virtually.

subEvents.title: Grand Finale: The Xcellence Showdown
subEvents.details: Finalists deliver disruptive, data-driven marketing presentations using advanced analytical models before an executive panel of judges. Concludes with a grand gala celebration and live prize award distribution.

title: AMD Developer Hackathon: ACT II
fee: Free (Requires registration to AMD AI Developer Program)
details: A hands-on online event focused on building AI agents and high-performance AI applications on AMD GPUs in the cloud using the AMD AI Developer Cloud, ROCm, and Fireworks AI API. Features a $15,000 total prize pool, plus perks for new AMD AI Developer Program sign-ups including $100 in AMD Cloud GPU credits and $50 in Fireworks AI API credits.

subEvents.title: Track 1: Hybrid Token-Efficient Routing Agent (Beginner AI Agent Track)
subEvents.details: Build an AI agent that completes tasks autonomously while minimizing token costs by choosing in real time between a local model and a remote model via Fireworks AI credits. Judging is based on token count and output accuracy.

subEvents.title: Track 2: Video Captioning (Beginner Friendly)
subEvents.details: Build a pipeline to generate captions/summaries for fixed short video clips (30s to 2min) in four distinct styles: formal, sarcastic, humorous-tech, and humorous-non-tech. Permitting fine-tuning and open datasets; evaluated by an LLM-Judge.

subEvents.title: Track 3: Unicorn Track (All Levels)
subEvents.details: Product- or startup-oriented track with no fixed performance benchmarks or constraints. Build using any open-source models/frameworks alongside AMD infrastructure. Evaluated on creativity, originality, completeness, and market potential.

title: Phoenix Summit 2026
venue: Bangabandhu International Conference Center (BICC), Dhaka, Bangladesh
fee:
Exhibition Pass: 200 BDT
Technical Pass (with Swag): 700 BDT
Student Workshops: 10,000 BDT
Professional Workshops: 15,000 BDT (Early Bird: 12,000 BDT)
details:
Phoenix Summit 2026 is Bangladesh's flagship cybersecurity convergence platform connecting government, industry, academia, and global experts.

From international instructor-led training and hands-on workshops to Red Team & Blue Team case studies, industry exhibitions, networking opportunities, and much more, Phoenix Summit 2026 promises an incredible learning and professional development experience.

subEvents.title: June 23-25: Pre-Summit Workshops (09:00 AM - 06:00 PM)
subEvents.details:
- Practical Cyber Investigation (Invite-only, APNIC)
- DevSecOps for Practitioners (Open)
- Master Application Security & Penetration Testing
- Ransomware Mechanics & Defensive Engineering

subEvents.details:
- 09:00 AM: Telecom Attack Detection Using AI (Pasan Maduranga)

- 05:40 PM: Real-Time IoC Detection with Wazuh (Imtiaz Rahman)

subEvents.details:
- 09:00 AM: AI in Offensive Security & Bug Bounty (Ayon Hasan)
- 09:30 AM: Exploiting JWTs & Library Gaps (Eftasib Araf Depro)
- 10:00 AM: Thailand's Cybersecurity Readiness (Paramin Chuangmanee)
